Axis#

Axis handling module.

This module defines the Axis class, representing a one-dimensional numeric axis typically used in scientific datasets (e.g., NetCDF files).

Example

>>> import numpy as np
>>> from netCDF4 import Dataset
>>> from axis import Axis
>>> time_axis = Axis("time", np.arange(0, 24),
...                  name="Time", units="hours since 2000-01-01 00:00:00")
>>> time_axis.info()
---------- axis id: time
---------- name: Time
---------- units: hours since 2000-01-01 00:00:00
---------- length: 24
>>> with Dataset("example.nc", "w") as nc:
...     axis.write(nc)
class Axis(axis_id, data, name=None, units=None, **kwargs)[source]#

Bases: object

Represents a numeric data axis, typically used in scientific datasets (e.g., NetCDF files).

Parameters:

  • axis_id (str) – Short name of the axis identifier (e.g., ‘time’, ‘lat’, ‘lon’).

  • data (array-like of float) – Sequence of numeric values for the axis. Will be converted to numpy.ndarray with dtype float64.

  • name (str, optional) – Human-readable name for the axis.

  • units (class:str, optional) – Units associated with the axis values.

  • **kwargs (dict, optional) –

    Additional arbitrary metadata. Each key/value pair is added as an attribute of the object and written to NetCDF as a variable attribute. For example:

    Axis("height", [0, 10, 20], name="altitude", units="m", source="radar")

    will create an Axis with a custom attribute source="radar".

Raises:

ValueError – If data is empty, None, or cannot be converted to a numeric NumPy array.

info(data=False)[source]#

Print metadata information about the axis.

Parameters:

data (bool, optional) – If True, also prints the full array of axis values. Default is False.

Returns:

Prints information to stdout.

Return type:

None

write(file_obj)[source]#

Write this axis as a dimension and variable to a NetCDF file.

Parameters:

file_obj (netCDF4.Dataset) – A NetCDF file opened in write or append mode.

Raises:

AttributeError – If file_obj does not support the required NetCDF API methods.

Returns:

Writes data and metadata into the provided NetCDF file object.

Return type:

None
